zh.lifeSummary: Mrs Roger Ingram於1270年之前與Roger Ingram在威爾士結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs Roger Ingram于1270年之前与Roger Ingram在威尔士结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Ingram
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman personal name Ingeram (Old French Enguerran, Engerran; ancient Germanic Engelramnus, Ingelramnus, Engelrammus, Ingelrammus), from the heroic name-element seen in such names as Ingle + hrafn- ‘raven’.

variants.name: Ingle
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Ingel, Ingald, Ingold (Old Norse Ingialdr, Ingœld, itself apparently borrowed from the Old English Ingeld, a name of ancient Germanic origin consisting of an intensive prefix in- + geld- ‘reward, forfeit’).

English: from the Middle English personal name Ingulf, representing either Old Norse Ingólfr, Ingulf or its ancient Germanic cognate, Ingenwulf, introduced to England by the Normans; the first element is derived from an ancient Germanic heroic name, and the second is ‘wolf’. As a surname it either died out or was absorbed by Ingle and its variants.
Americanized form of German Ingel: from a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names formed with Ing- (see 1 above). The surname Ingel is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Ingraham
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): of Norman origin, a variant of   Ingram  , influenced by   Graham  . This name is now more common in the US than in Britain.

variants.name: Engram
meanings: English: variant of   Ingram  . This form, recorded inthe 13th century, now appears to have died out in the British Isles.

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Roger Ingram married Roger Ingram before 1270, in Wales. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs Roger Ingram a épousé Roger Ingram avant 1270, à Galles. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs Roger Ingram hat vor 1270 in Wales Roger Ingram geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs Roger Ingram lei sposato Roger Ingram prima del 1270, nel Galles. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Mrs Roger Ingramは1270年以前にウェールズでRoger Ingramと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs Roger Ingram(은)는 1270년 이전 웨일스에서 Roger Ingram(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs Roger Ingram casou-se com Roger Ingram antes 1270, em Gales. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs Roger Ingram вышла замуж за человека по имени Roger Ingram перед 1270 г. в местности Уэльс. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs Roger Ingram se casó con Roger Ingram antes de 1270, en Gales. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
